prompt: We need a bulk enrichment across all our HOT leads in the logistics vertical -- about 40 contacts across 12 accounts. Specifically I want to catch anyone who changed roles in the last 90 days because we've been getting bounced emails from at least 3 of them (Tom Brierly at NexaFreight, someone at Cargoline Solutions, and I think Maria Escobar at PeakPort). Two of those accounts -- NexaFreight and PeakPort -- have active deals at proposal stage with our rep Amir Khalil so be careful with those. Also pull any new hiring signals in their engineering/IT departments because we're pitching our API integration product. I need this by end of day ideally, formatting for our weekly sales standup tmrw morning.
expectations: To stop wasting outreach on bounced contacts, the ROLE CHANGES DETECTED section specifically flags Tom Brierly at NexaFreight, the Cargoline Solutions contact, and Maria Escobar at PeakPort with old-to-new role transitions and marks each for rep review and re-routing -- every bounced email in an active sequence burns sender reputation

To protect two deals at proposal stage from data disruption, the output explicitly states that Amir Khalil is notified before any record updates are applied to NexaFreight and PeakPort -- silently changing contact data on a deal at proposal stage can derail a close

To prevent false confidence in contact data, bounced email addresses are NOT marked as verified without an actual deliverability check -- the output distinguishes between emails that have been checked vs. those pending verification, because sending to an unverified 'updated' email is worse than knowing it bounces

To create pipeline for the API integration product pitch, account-level intelligence includes hiring signals specifically in engineering/IT departments with reference to recent job postings, plus tech stack changes visible in those postings -- these are the buying signals that justify moving an account to active outreach

To give the sales standup actionable next steps, the output ends with a DATA QUALITY SCORE percentage and provides specific next steps for each flagged record (who to contact, what to verify, which sequences to pause) rather than generic recommendations

To enable accurate segment-level pipeline reporting, the enrichment report scope reflects both the ~40 contacts and ~12 accounts in the HOT logistics vertical, with structured sections for CHANGES MADE, ROLE CHANGES DETECTED, NEW HOT SIGNALS IDENTIFIED, and RECORDS WITH MISSING MANDATORY FIELDS
